affordances.name: Favored framework blindness check
affordances.mechanism: When a favored theory or model is shaping what counts as relevant, require the answer to ask what the current framework filters out and whether another model would expose missing signal.

use_when: A favored framework might be hiding reality or filtering inconvenient evidence.
Different models would produce materially different cuts through the problem.
Progress depends on exposing what the dominant theory is causing everyone not to notice.
do_not_use_when: The current framework's assumptions, omissions, and limits are already explicit.
The answer would keep shopping for theories to avoid evidence, testable claims, or operational decisions.
The model is used to reject all frameworks instead of checking whether this framework fits.
case_evidence_needed: The framework, theory, or model currently shaping the advice.
The assumptions and variables that framework foregrounds.
The evidence or variables the framework may be filtering out.
The alternative cut that could expose missing signal.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the favored framework, what it makes visible, what it filters out, and what alternative cut would test whether the current map is hiding reality.
evidence_required: The current model or theory behind the advice.
The assumptions embedded in that model.
The missing or inconvenient evidence the model may filter out.
A plausible alternative frame that would expose different signal.
good_output_shape: Names the current framework rather than attacking expertise generally.
Identifies what the framework ignores or abstracts away.
Offers an alternative cut only if it changes evidence or action.

diagnostic_questions: What theory or framework is deciding what counts as relevant here?
What evidence would this framework make easy to ignore?
Which alternative cut would expose signal the current model is hiding?
misuse_guards: Do not treat all frameworks as suspect; test fit, omissions, and assumptions.
Do not keep changing theories to avoid operational evidence or a decision.
Do not let a favored model absorb contradictory evidence without revision.

source_evidence.source_quote: Most useful when a favored framework might be hiding reality
Works when different models yield materially different cuts
Best when reframing can uncover missing signal

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 10 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports a favored-framework blindness card that asks what a model filters out.
dropped_material: Did not extract a broad mental-models or latticework card.
Did not extract AI personality consistency material because PR45 is not runtime or prompt work.
open_questions: PR46 should test whether this card helps packets catch framework lock-in without encouraging theory shopping.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that changing frameworks can become misuse when it avoids commitment to evidence, testable claims, or operational decisions.

source_evidence.source_quote: Danger When Reframing Becomes Endless Theory Shopping

absence_records.reason: The source says models necessarily omit parts of reality, so the record must not let a useful framework become a complete representation of the territory.

source_evidence.source_quote: they "do not embrace every aspect of reality"
A way of seeing is also a way of not seeing

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 10 source reading found strong reviewed depth for theory-induced-blindness as a favored-framework blindness check.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether the card exposes missing signal without making the LLM distrust every framework.
